file_insights.purpose: GitHub Actions CI pipeline for E-Menum project
key_components: Workflow triggers on push and pull requests to main and develop branches
Environment variables configured for testing (Node 20, PostgreSQL, Redis, JWT secrets)
Concurrency settings to cancel in-progress runs
Five parallel/sequential job stages: lint-and-type-check, unit-tests, integration-tests, build, e2e-tests

patterns_discovered.description: Workflow uses needs field to establish job dependencies: lint-and-type-check runs first, then unit-tests and integration-tests in parallel, build depends on both test jobs, e2e-tests depends on build and only runs on main branch push
PostgreSQL and Redis services configured with health checks in integration-tests and e2e-tests jobs to ensure readiness before running tests
Four distinct testing levels: linting/type-checking, unit tests with coverage, integration tests with database, and e2e tests with full environment
Build artifacts uploaded with 7-day retention, e2e test results uploaded only on failure
Centralized env block at workflow level provides common variables (NODE_VERSION, database URLs, JWT secrets) accessible across all jobs
Three quality checks before testing: ESLint, TypeScript type checking, and formatting verification

gotchas_discovered.description: Database and JWT secrets are hardcoded in workflow file (test:test, test-secret-key-for-ci-only). While acceptable for test environments, these should use GitHub Secrets in production environments
E2E tests only run on main branch pushes (if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'), meaning pull requests and develop branch pushes skip e2e validation
Codecov upload configured with fail_ci_if_error: false, allowing CI to pass even if coverage upload fails
Workflow assumes 'npx prisma migrate deploy' will work without errors, but doesn't have error handling or rollback strategy for failed migrations

key_achievements: Comprehensive CI pipeline with 5 job stages covering linting, testing, building, and e2e validation
Efficient use of job dependencies and concurrency to minimize runtime
Proper service container configuration with health checks for PostgreSQL and Redis
Code coverage integration with Codecov for unit and integration tests
Artifact retention strategy for build outputs and test results

recommendations.recommendation: Move hardcoded secrets to GitHub Secrets. Replace JWT_SECRET and JWT_REFRESH_SECRET inline values with ${{ secrets.JWT_SECRET }} and ${{ secrets.JWT_REFRESH_SECRET }}
recommendations.rationale: Even test credentials should follow security best practices and not be committed to repository

recommendations.recommendation: Extend e2e-tests to run on develop branch pushes or all pull requests, not just main branch
recommendations.rationale: Develop branch deserves same e2e validation as main to catch integration issues earlier

recommendations.recommendation: Add error handling for database migrations with explicit failure message if migrations fail
recommendations.rationale: Migrations are critical path and should fail fast with clear diagnostics

recommendations.recommendation: Add workflow_dispatch trigger to allow manual CI runs for debugging or on-demand validation
recommendations.rationale: Manual trigger provides flexibility for ad-hoc testing without pushing code

recommendations.recommendation: Consider caching node_modules more aggressively or using npm ci with --prefer-offline flag for faster builds
recommendations.rationale: All five jobs reinstall dependencies; caching layer could reduce redundant downloads
